text.title: Training Data Sourcing Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Training Data Sourcing Policy application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Policy defines a closed allowed-list of source classes (vendor-provided / public-domain / opt-in customer corpus); any other source is rejected.

rationale.source: Training Data Sourcing Policy authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Open enums lead to ad-hoc scraping; a closed list is auditable.

rule.statement: PII is redacted or pseudonymised before training unless explicit consent recorded; default deny.

rationale.source: Training Data Sourcing Policy authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Training on un-redacted PII creates extraction-attack exposure; default-deny is the only safe baseline.

rule.statement: Every row in the corpus has a licence tag traceable to the source manifest; rows without tag are dropped.

rationale.source: Training Data Sourcing Policy authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Untagged rows leak unlicensed content into weights; the trail is the audit anchor.

rule.statement: Customer-corpus opt-in carries an expiry; re-consent required for runs >365 days after collection.

rationale.source: Training Data Sourcing Policy authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Stale consent is invalid consent; the 365-day window matches privacy-rights norms.

rule.statement: Each fine-tune run names an approver who signs off on the corpus + the policy version applied.

rationale.source: Training Data Sourcing Policy authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Plural approval = no approval; named approver enables incident attribution.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
